content:
[S1]: Inside every living thing, there are tiny workers called cells that do important jobs to keep us alive.  
[READER]: That sounds interesting! I know cells are small, but I want to know what kind of jobs they do next.

[S2]: One very important job is making proteins, which help our bodies grow, heal, and stay strong.  
[READER]: Oh, proteins sound important! But how do cells know how to make these proteins?

[S3]: mRNA is like a special message that carries instructions from the cell’s control center to the places where proteins are made.  
[READER]: Hmm, a message? Like a letter or a secret code? I want to understand how this message works.

[S4]: The cell’s control center, called the nucleus, has a big book of instructions called DNA.  
[READER]: A big book? That sounds cool! Is the mRNA copying from this book?

[S5]: Yes! mRNA copies just one small instruction from the DNA book and takes it outside the nucleus.  
[READER]: So mRNA is like a copy machine? That makes sense! What happens when it gets outside?

[S6]: Outside the nucleus, the mRNA delivers its message to tiny machines called ribosomes that read the instructions.  
[READER]: Machines that read? Like robots? I want to know what the ribosomes do with the instructions.

[S7]: The ribosomes follow the mRNA’s instructions to put together building blocks and make a protein.  
[READER]: Wow, so the ribosomes are like chefs following a recipe! What kind of proteins do they make?

[S8]: Proteins can be things like muscles to help you move or special helpers to fight germs and keep you healthy.  
[READER]: That’s amazing! Now I understand how the message from mRNA helps my body stay strong and healthy.  

[S9]: And that’s how mRNA carries important instructions inside your cells to keep everything working just right!  
[READER]: I like that! It sounds like mRNA is a tiny messenger hero inside me.  

[S10]: Do you want to learn more about how other parts of the cell work too?  
[READER]: Yes, definitely! I’m curious to know more about the cell’s tiny helpers!
